title: phaseolus semierectus var. atropurpureus
intro: Phaseolus semierectus var. atropurpureus, also known as purple bean or purple kidney bean, is a variant of the Phaseolus semierectus plant, which is a type of climbing legume native to Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of purple bean include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially lowering the risk of certain cancers. Purple bean is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as teas and decoctions, which can be consumed fresh or dried, and is also used in traditional Asian medicine to support overall health and well-being.
